title: New Fans

jokes.text: Where's yours? - It's on my chair.
jokes.setup: Murray asks about Bret's leather jacket for image
jokes.punchline: Bret's jacket is literally just sitting on his chair, not being worn
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character comedy showing Bret's passive nature and disconnect from image concerns

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Where's yours?

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: It's on my chair.

jokes.text: you could have a bit of hay coming out of your mouth... a little bit of hay. - All the time? - Yeah.
jokes.punchline: The specificity of 'a little bit of hay' and Jemaine's earnest follow-up about timing
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Murray's misguided management style and the band's earnest consideration of absurd advice

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: you could have a bit of hay coming out of your mouth... a little bit of hay.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: All the time?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Yeah.

jokes.text: 'Cause there was probably only one entry. - No, there was hundreds, actually. - Really? - Yep. They're all from Mel.
jokes.punchline: The reveal that hundreds of entries are all from the same obsessed fan
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection with perfect timing, establishing Mel's obsessive fandom

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: 'Cause there was probably only one entry.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: No, there was hundreds, actually.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Yep. They're all from Mel.

jokes.text: And one from Jemaine. - Right. - But he was disqualified.
jokes.punchline: Band member entered his own band's fan contest and was disqualified
jokes.explanation: Shows Jemaine's earnest enthusiasm contrasted with Murray's business logic

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: And one from Jemaine.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Right.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: But he was disqualified.

jokes.text: Because you can't be a fan of the band. - Why? - It's not a good look.
jokes.setup: Jemaine asking why he can't be a fan
jokes.punchline: Murray's serious concern about optics of band members liking their own band
jokes.explanation: Murray's misguided business logic creates absurd situation

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Because you can't be a fan of the band.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Why?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: It's not a good look.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, that's 'cause I'm not a fan of the band. I'm more a fan of popular bands like the Bee Gees, Pearl Jam.
jokes.setup: Discussion of who's on the fan list
jokes.punchline: Bret openly admits he's not a fan of his own band, prefers successful bands
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Bret's brutal honesty about their lack of success

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, that's 'cause I'm not a fan of the band. I'm more a fan of popular bands like the Bee Gees, Pearl Jam.

jokes.text: First prize... the chance to cook for you two tomorrow night. - That's a terrible prize. I'm glad I didn't win that.
jokes.punchline: Jemaine immediately recognizes how awful the 'prize' is
jokes.explanation: Highlights the band's lack of desirability and Murray's poor management

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: First prize... the chance to cook for you two tomorrow night.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: That's a terrible prize. I'm glad I didn't win that.

jokes.text: I've already e-mailed her pretending to be you saying you're excited. - Were you pretending to be me as well? - Yes, that's what I do. It's... it's easier.
jokes.punchline: Murray casually admits to impersonating both band members because it's 'easier'
jokes.explanation: Shows Murray's lazy management style and complete disregard for authenticity

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: I've already e-mailed her pretending to be you saying you're excited.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Were you pretending to be me as well?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Yes, that's what I do. It's... it's easier.

jokes.text: Actually, next time you see Mel, you might want to apologize for your last e-mail. - Okay. Why? - It's just, I think she was quite insulted.
jokes.punchline: Implication that Murray sent an insulting email while pretending to be Jemaine
jokes.explanation: Creates awkward situation where Jemaine must apologize for something he didn't do

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Actually, next time you see Mel, you might want to apologize for your last e-mail.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Okay. Why?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: It's just, I think she was quite insulted.

jokes.text: Bonjour, lay ho, salaam, nabadeey, konnichiwa, and hola. Welcome to Tuesday World Music Jam.
jokes.punchline: Overly enthusiastic multicultural greeting that sounds forced and artificial
jokes.explanation: Mocks performative multiculturalism of world music events

dialogue.speaker: MC
dialogue.line: Bonjour, lay ho, salaam, nabadeey, konnichiwa, and hola. Welcome to Tuesday World Music Jam.

jokes.text: World Music Jam? We don't play world music. Do we? - Yes. Where's New Zealand from if it's not from the world?
jokes.setup: Bret questioning their classification as world music
jokes.punchline: Murray's literal interpretation that all music is technically 'world music'
jokes.explanation: Murray's earnest but flawed logic about geography and music categorization

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: World Music Jam? We don't play world music. Do we?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Yes. Where's New Zealand from if it's not from the world?

jokes.text: There's a lot of hot women here. - Yeah. - Look at those two. - Whoa. Whew.
jokes.setup: Noticing attractive women at the venue
jokes.punchline: Their awkward, overly enthusiastic reactions
jokes.explanation: Sets up their awkwardness around women, though fairly standard setup

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: There's a lot of hot women here.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Look at those two.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Whoa. Whew.

jokes.text: Ladies song with increasingly absurd nationality descriptors culminating in 'Amphibian Lady' and 'Presbyterian Lady'
jokes.setup: Song celebrating women from different countries
jokes.punchline: List escalates from real nationalities to ridiculous categories like amphibian and religious denominations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from legitimate to absurd, showcasing the show's musical comedy strength

dialogue.line: Ladies song with increasingly absurd nationality descriptors culminating in 'Amphibian Lady' and 'Presbyterian Lady'

jokes.text: Hermaphrodite Lady-man lady / All you sexy hermaphrodite lady-man ladies
jokes.punchline: Attempting political correctness but creating confusing compound terms
jokes.explanation: Awkward attempt at inclusivity that becomes linguistically tangled

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Hermaphrodite Lady-man lady / All you sexy hermaphrodite lady-man ladies

jokes.text: If every soldier in the world / Put down his weapon and picked up a woman / What a peaceful world this world would be
jokes.punchline: Treating women as objects to be 'picked up' instead of weapons
jokes.explanation: Subverts peace anthem trope with unintentionally problematic imagery

dialogue.line: If every soldier in the world / Put down his weapon and picked up a woman / What a peaceful world this world would be

jokes.text: Redheads not warheads / Blondes not bombs / We're talking about brunettes not fighter jets
jokes.punchline: Increasingly forced rhymes, especially 'brunettes/fighter jets'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that gets progressively more strained

dialogue.line: Redheads not warheads / Blondes not bombs / We're talking about brunettes not fighter jets

jokes.text: Sweet 16s not M-16s
jokes.punchline: Connecting sweet sixteen parties to assault rifles
jokes.explanation: Sharp wordplay with dark undertones, very quotable

dialogue.line: Sweet 16s not M-16s

jokes.text: Where every lady gets a little piece of Bretty
jokes.punchline: Reduces global harmony to personal sexual fantasy
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Bret's narcissistic worldview

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Where every lady gets a little piece of Bretty

jokes.text: I don't care if you're ugly or you're skanky or you're small / Just want to do a little something special for you all
jokes.setup: Attempting to be inclusive and romantic
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliments that insult while claiming acceptance
jokes.explanation: Shows Bret's inability to be genuinely complimentary

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: I don't care if you're ugly or you're skanky or you're small / Just want to do a little something special for you all

jokes.text: Something completely different here. They're from New Zealand. We have... The Flute of the Commodores.
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong band name mixing 'Flight' with 'Flute of the'
jokes.explanation: Shows their lack of recognition even when performing

dialogue.speaker: MC
dialogue.line: Something completely different here. They're from New Zealand. We have... The Flute of the Commodores.

jokes.text: Song opening with extensive tuning: A, B... A... D, G, G, G... G, G, G... G...
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly long tuning session performed as if it's part of the act
jokes.explanation: Authentic amateur musician cringe moment that goes on uncomfortably long

dialogue.line: Song opening with extensive tuning: A, B... A... D, G, G, G... G, G, G... G...

jokes.text: Who likes to rock the party? / I like to rock the party
jokes.setup: Attempting to engage crowd with call and response
jokes.punchline: Extremely simple, repetitive lyrics that lack any sophistication
jokes.explanation: Shows their simplistic approach to crowd-pleasing

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Who likes to rock the party?

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: I like to rock the party

jokes.text: Different start to the song. - What were you doing there? - No, it's the same.
jokes.punchline: Bret's oblivious insistence that their obviously different tuning was 'the same'
jokes.explanation: Bret's lack of self-awareness about their performance

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Different start to the song.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: What were you doing there?

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: No, it's the same.

jokes.text: I bought you guys a beer. - I don't even drink beer. - I don't drink it either. - Just drink it, will you?
jokes.setup: Murray buying them beers for image
jokes.punchline: Both refuse because they don't actually drink beer
jokes.explanation: Shows conflict between authentic selves and rock image

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: I bought you guys a beer.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: I don't even drink beer.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I don't drink it either.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Just drink it, will you?

jokes.text: I do things for my image... make it more rock 'n' roll with the goatee and everything. I don't like this goatee. I hate it. - Makes you look like a goat, doesn't it? - Yeah, exactly.
jokes.punchline: Goatee literally making him look like a goat, which he acknowledges
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay on goatee/goat appearance

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I do things for my image... make it more rock 'n' roll with the goatee and everything. I don't like this goatee. I hate it.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Makes you look like a goat, doesn't it?

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Yeah, exactly.

jokes.text: Tell you what, maybe if I pretend to go to the toilet, you guys see if they're looking at me. - Okay? - Yep.
jokes.setup: Jemaine wanting to test if women are interested
jokes.punchline: Elaborate scheme to determine female attention through fake bathroom trip
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward male behavior around potential romantic interests

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Tell you what, maybe if I pretend to go to the toilet, you guys see if they're looking at me.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Okay?

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Yep.

jokes.text: No, they're not looking at me! I might actually go to the toilet now that I'm over here!
jokes.setup: Jemaine's scheme to test female interest
jokes.punchline: Disappointed announcement followed by practical decision to use bathroom anyway
jokes.explanation: Shows Jemaine's pragmatic response to romantic disappointment

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: No, they're not looking at me! I might actually go to the toilet now that I'm over here!

jokes.text: You must have been really wasted when you wrote that. - No. - Do you mean on drugs? - Oh, yes, we were tripping when we wrote that one, that's right.
jokes.setup: Fan asking about their creative process
jokes.punchline: Jemaine initially honest, then immediately changes to seem more rock and roll
jokes.explanation: Shows Jemaine's desperation to appear cool and rock-like

dialogue.line: You must have been really wasted when you wrote that.

dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Fan
dialogue.line: Do you mean on drugs?

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Oh, yes, we were tripping when we wrote that one, that's right.

jokes.text: We were totally whacked off. - What? - Whacked off? - Uh, that's just a drug term we use back in New Zealand
jokes.punchline: Uses sexual term thinking it means drugs, then doubles down with fake explanation
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe comedy from cultural misunderstanding and doubling down on mistake

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: We were totally whacked off.

dialogue.line: Whacked off?

jokes.text: I got a shirt that's got a mouse having sex with another mouse in a mousetrap. I score chicks in this shirt all the time.
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate shirt design that he claims is successful with women
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and disturbing image that perfectly captures Dave's misguided confidence

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: I got a shirt that's got a mouse having sex with another mouse in a mousetrap. I score chicks in this shirt all the time.

jokes.text: I guarantee you, I was just pumping away in this thing the other night.
jokes.punchline: Crude description of sexual success attributed to the shirt
jokes.explanation: Dave's crude overconfidence, though somewhat predictable

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: I guarantee you, I was just pumping away in this thing the other night.

jokes.text: I was hoping to dress something like Prince. Kind of erogenous, huh? - Yeah, but toned down a bit, like, Prince if he was just going to the zoo or the supermarket. - Casual Prince?
jokes.punchline: Specific scenario of Prince doing mundane activities, 'Casual Prince'
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist concept that's highly specific and quotable

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: I was hoping to dress something like Prince. Kind of erogenous, huh?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Yeah, but toned down a bit, like, Prince if he was just going to the zoo or the supermarket.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Casual Prince?

jokes.text: Jesus! Sorry guys. My roommate's... being kind of a jerk.
jokes.punchline: Dave dismissing what's clearly his mother as a 'jerk roommate'
jokes.explanation: Sets up Dave's elaborate denial about living with parents

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: Jesus! Sorry guys. My roommate's... being kind of a jerk.

jokes.text: Is that a woman's blouse? - No. - Looks like a blouse. - No no no. This is what you're looking for, man. That's totally Prince.
jokes.punchline: Obviously feminine clothing that Dave insists is masculine and Prince-like
jokes.explanation: Shows Dave's questionable fashion sense and gender understanding

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Is that a woman's blouse?

dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Looks like a blouse.

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: No no no. This is what you're looking for, man. That's totally Prince.

jokes.text: Dude, you think I live with my parents? - Oh, is that your dad, though? - No. That's just some old, crotchety Indian couple that I sublet to.
jokes.setup: Dave denying he lives with parents
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate explanation about subletting to an Indian couple
jokes.explanation: Dave's desperation leads to increasingly absurd explanations

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Dude, you think I live with my parents?

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: Oh, is that your dad, though?

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: No. That's just some old, crotchety Indian couple that I sublet to.

jokes.text: The weird thing is they look like me. They've got photos of themselves with you as a kid. - I know. It's creepy. I think they make them on the computer.
jokes.setup: Bret pointing out obvious family photos
jokes.punchline: Dave's conspiracy theory about computer-generated fake family photos
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing complete disconnect from reality in service of maintaining lie

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: The weird thing is they look like me. They've got photos of themselves with you as a kid.

dialogue.line: I know. It's creepy. I think they make them on the computer.

jokes.text: That's... that's our bedroom. - Yep. That's a live webcam from your bedroom.
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of privacy invasion through bedroom webcam
jokes.explanation: Murray's casual violation of privacy for fan engagement

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: That's... that's our bedroom.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Yep. That's a live webcam from your bedroom.

jokes.text: I've had a really good response from something that happened with you when you were alone with the webcam. Do you know what that would be?
jokes.punchline: Implication of embarrassing private behavior caught on camera
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable implication about private moments being broadcast

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: I've had a really good response from something that happened with you when you were alone with the webcam. Do you know what that would be?

jokes.text: I didn't know we had a webcam. - Okay, well, whatever you're doing, just be careful, all right?

dialogue.line: I didn't know we had a webcam.

dialogue.line: Okay, well, whatever you're doing, just be careful, all right?

jokes.text: We've tripled the fan base. So we need two new t-shirts made up.
jokes.punchline: Going from one fan to three fans requiring only two shirts

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: We've tripled the fan base. So we need two new t-shirts made up.

jokes.text: You'll end up like ZZ Top. - What are you talking about? - You know, ZZ Top. - Yeah? - They sang the song about the... the... the woman with the legs.

jokes.text: That's nice, like... kind of like bad weather.

dialogue.line: That's nice, like... kind of like bad weather.

jokes.text: Do you not have a computer, or... - No, I do have a computer. - Oh, you can't read.

dialogue.line: Do you not have a computer, or...

jokes.text: Oh, I thought he was your dad. - He's a little bit older than me. - Yeah. - He was my college professor.
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of inappropriate student-teacher relationship
jokes.explanation: Mel's oblivious sharing of inappropriate relationship details

dialogue.speaker: Rain
dialogue.line: Oh, I thought he was your dad.

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: He's a little bit older than me.

dialogue.speaker: Rain
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: He was my college professor.

jokes.text: He resisted for a while and there were some legal... boundaries, you know, keeping me from being near him or... his family, but... in the end, love overcame.
jokes.punchline: Casual description of restraining orders presented as romantic obstacles
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy from Mel's romanticized view of stalking behavior

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: He resisted for a while and there were some legal... boundaries, you know, keeping me from being near him or... his family, but... in the end, love overcame.

jokes.text: I always get what I want.
jokes.punchline: Ominous statement suggesting obsessive persistence
jokes.explanation: Menacing undertone to Mel's obsessive personality

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: I always get what I want.

jokes.text: But you don't drink beer, Bret. - I drink it all the time. - I thought it always made you go to the toilet.

jokes.text: What, for the manager to e-mail the fans and offer them man-man-lady threesome?

jokes.explanation: Absurd specificity about management overreach

jokes.text: You'll end up going into town in a taxi, have a couple of drugs, have dinner, have a threesome, go home again. Have a shower, go out again, more drugs, more threesomes... happens all the time.
jokes.punchline: Mundane, scheduled approach to debauchery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant business-casual description of rock and roll excess

tmdb_overview: A performance at a local "World Music Jam" results in the Conchords gaining two new fans, but Mel is suspicious of the newcomers and their motives. Murray encourages the band to adopt a more "rock star" attitude.
